issues.description: Exponential time complexity O(2^n) due to recursive implementation without memoization
No input validation for negative numbers or non-integer values
Missing docstring to explain function purpose, parameters, and return value
No type hints for function parameters and return value
Risk of stack overflow for large values of n due to deep recursion

strengths: Simple and clean implementation that directly follows mathematical definition
Easy to understand logic for beginners
weaknesses: Extremely inefficient for large values of n
No error handling or input validation
Missing type annotations for better code clarity

improvements_suggested.reasoning: Eliminates stack overflow risk and improves space complexity to O(1)

optimization_opportunities.impact: Reduces time complexity from O(2^n) to O(n), making n=40 computation time drop from ~10 seconds to microseconds
Reduces space complexity from O(n) to O(1) and eliminates recursion limit issues
Can achieve O(log n) time complexity for very large n values

security_concerns.concern: Denial of Service vulnerability through stack exhaustion
security_concerns.description: Large input values can cause stack overflow, potentially crashing the application
Function accepts any input type without validation, could lead to unexpected behavior

result.optimized_code:
def fibonacci(n: int) -> int:
    """
    Calculate the nth Fibonacci number using dynamic programming.
    
    Args:
        n: The position of the Fibonacci number to calculate (0-indexed)
        
    Returns:
        The nth Fibonacci number
        
    Raises:
        ValueError: If n is negative
        TypeError: If n is not an integer
    """
    if not isinstance(n, int):
        raise TypeError(f"Expected integer, got {type(n).__name__}")
    if n < 0:
        raise ValueError("Fibonacci number position must be non-negative")
    
    if n <= 1:
        return n
    
    # Use iterative approach with O(1) space
    prev, curr = 0, 1
    for _ in range(2, n + 1):
        prev, curr = curr, prev + curr
    
    return curr

optimizations_made.optimization: Replaced recursive approach with iterative dynamic programming
optimizations_made.explanation: Eliminates redundant calculations and reduces time complexity from O(2^n) to O(n)
Instead of storing all Fibonacci numbers in an array, only keep track of the last two values, reducing space complexity from O(n) to O(1)
Checks for negative numbers and non-integer inputs to prevent unexpected behavior
Improves code clarity and enables better IDE support and static type checking
Documents function purpose, parameters, return value, and exceptions for better maintainability
Iterative approach prevents stack overflow errors for large values of n

original.time: O(2^n) - Exponential due to redundant recursive calculations
original.space: O(n) - Due to recursion call stack depth

optimized.time: O(n) - Linear, single pass through range
optimized.space: O(1) - Constant, only storing two variables

result.improvement_summary: Transformed exponential time complexity to linear time with constant space usage, while adding robust error handling and documentation
trade_offs: Slightly more complex code structure compared to the elegant recursive solution
Lost the mathematical clarity of the recursive definition
Added overhead for input validation (negligible for performance)

positive: Better error handling and user feedback
neutral: More lines of code but better structured
Iterative approach may be less intuitive for some developers

performance_metrics.space_improvement: 100% reduction in auxiliary space (from O(n) to O(1))
performance_metrics.scalability: Can now handle n>10000 without stack overflow

test_cases.name: test_fibonacci_small_positive_numbers
test_cases.code:
def test_fibonacci_small_positive_numbers():
    assert fibonacci(2) == 1
    assert fibonacci(3) == 2
    assert fibonacci(4) == 3
    assert fibonacci(5) == 5
    assert fibonacci(6) == 8
    assert fibonacci(7) == 13
test_cases.description: Tests fibonacci sequence for small positive integers

test_cases.name: test_fibonacci_negative_numbers
test_fibonacci_large_number_recursion
test_cases.code:
def test_fibonacci_large_number_recursion():
    import sys
    old_limit = sys.getrecursionlimit()
    sys.setrecursionlimit(100)
    try:
        with pytest.raises(RecursionError):
            fibonacci(150)
    finally:
        sys.setrecursionlimit(old_limit)
test_cases.description: Tests that large inputs cause recursion error

test_cases.name: test_fibonacci_type_error
test_cases.code:
def test_fibonacci_type_error():
    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        fibonacci('5')
    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        fibonacci(5.5)
    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        fibonacci(None)
    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        fibonacci([])
test_cases.description: Tests that non-integer inputs raise TypeError

test_cases.name: test_fibonacci_sequence_property
test_cases.description: Tests that the fibonacci property F(n) = F(n-1) + F(n-2) holds

test_cases.name: test_fibonacci_known_values
